article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged quoted/source-attributed language as author bias, used non-allowed bias type labels, and included a questionable claim of imbalance. The article’s author prose is largely neutral and attributive, so these are largely false positives.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Two of three items are direct quotes or clearly attributed statements flagged as bias, violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All flagged items are not genuine author-level bias; two are quotes and one is a neutral disclosure about outreach to companies.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: No clear, significant bias in the author’s connecting prose appears to be missed; the article largely attributes claims.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels used (Loaded Language, Imbalance of Sources, Appeal to Authority) are outside the allowed set and misapplied.
